project.title: Faema E61 Legend
project.services: Full restoration, Chrome replating, Boiler rebuild
project.overview: A 1961 Faema E61 that had been sitting in the back of a closed bar for the better part of fifteen years. This is the machine that introduced the now famous E61 group and changed how espresso was pulled, so getting it right mattered. It came in seized, scaled and dull, but the bones were all there. The goal was a full restoration that respected the original engineering while making the machine genuinely usable again behind a working counter.
project.objective: The owners wanted a centrepiece for their reopened bar, not a museum display. So the brief was to keep everything that made the E61 what it is, the lever, the chrome, the proportions, while quietly bringing the internals up to a standard where it could be run every day. Nothing modern was to be visible. Anyone looking at it should see a 1961 machine. Anyone using it should get a machine that performs like new.
project.process: I started by stripping the machine down to the last component and cataloguing everything. The boiler was descaled and pressure tested, the heating element replaced, and every seal and gasket renewed with correct specification parts. The group was rebuilt and the lever action freed up and re-greased so it moves the way it was meant to. The chrome was the slow part. Decades of pitting meant the major panels had to go out for replating, and I spent a good while hand polishing the brass and copper underneath. The wiring was completely renewed for safety, hidden behind original-looking cloth where it shows. The body panels were repainted in the correct period red, colour matched against an unfaded section I found underneath the base.
project.impact: The Faema now sits at the heart of the reopened Caffè Roma, pulling shots every day. It holds pressure perfectly, the lever is smooth, and the chrome catches the light the way it would have sixty years ago. Regulars have started asking about it, which was exactly the point. A machine like this is part of the room, and giving one back to a working bar rather than a private collection is the kind of job I most like to take on.
